rulings.Case Title: TRAVIS BRYANT ET AL VS. NICHOLAS SPENCER FIRTH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Of Litigation As To Moving Defendants
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 5, 2025 line 1. DEFENDANT RISHI GARG INDIVIDUALLY AND AS TRUSTEE OF THE PRAKASH FAMILY TRUST DATED NOVEMBER 15, 2017, SHAIFALI PRAKASH Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Of Litigation As To Moving Defendants; **First Appearance Fees Paid** is GRANTED. Claims against the seller shall proceed in arbitration. There is a binding arbitration agreement between the parties that has not been waived. Based on the allegations in the Complaint, there isn't sufficient information to determine that there is an overlap between the allegations of defects and failure to disclose those same defects (if known to the seller), warranting a finding that inconsistent ruling may result from proceeding in two forums. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SF 701 TAYLOR ST, LLC VS. AMY PHILLIPS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Enforcement Of Settlement Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 5, 2025 line 3. OTHER SF OWNER LLC Motion For Enforcement Of Settlement Agreement is DENIED. rulings.Case Title: RENEE POIRIER-POUND VS. CARROL TORRES ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 5, 2025 line 4. PLAINTIFF RENEE POIRIER-POUND MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Moving party failed to carry its burden and show that there are no triable issues of fact as to the elements of her unlawful detainer case. Plaintiff point to no evidence showing that Graciella Torres either was a person subject to the writ of execution or that she is "a person claiming under" someone who was subject to the writ. CCP 1161a. Motion for Summary Adjudication is GRANTED. Defendant provides no admissible evidence to support any of her affirmative defenses. See defendant's responses to discovery and declaration in support of opposition. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: JAMES JOWDY VS. CHRISTINA DIAZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Motion
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 5, 2025 line 7. PLAINTIFF JAMES JOWDY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Motion is not considered at this time. In opposition Defendant asked for consolidation of this action with a pending civil action. Plaintiff filed a reply to Defendant's opposition, which did not contain any objection to the request to consolidate. Therefore, the Court will grant consolidation. For the duration of the consolidation Dependent Diaz will be required to post fair market rental value for both units subject to this action on the first of each month. The Court will determine the fair market value at the hearing. Both sides to present evidence of the fair market value of the property at the hearing. Any written evidence to be filed and served no later than 9:00 a.m. on February 5, 2025. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
